items.title: Thicker polyethylene inserts for varus deformity versus thinner inserts for flexion contracture in total knee arthroplasty: a retrospective comparative study
items.description: CONCLUSIONS: Preoperative HKA angle and FC were identified as significant predictors of PE insert thickness in TKA. Level of evidence Level III, retrospective comparative study.

items.description: Vascular remodeling, the adaptive reshaping of the vascular network in response to changing demands of the tissue, plays a critical role in embryo development and various pathologies. items.title: Predictive processing in time perception: Assessing prediction error minimization in the sub-second range
items.description: The predictive processing theory proposes that minimization of prediction error is a governing principle of perception, learning, and behavior, yet few studies have directly tested this hypothesis in time perception. items.title: Mid-term outcomes of posterior mitral leaflet extension using fresh autologous pericardium for pediatric mitral regurgitation
items.description: CONCLUSIONS: Posterior mitral leaflet extension using fresh autologous pericardium may serve as a viable surgical option for selected pediatric patients with MR associated with posterior leaflet shortening or tethering.

items.title: Clinical outcomes and prognostic factors of open-window thoracostomy for empyema: a retrospective review of 40 patients
items.description: CONCLUSIONS: The preoperative nutritional status, systemic inflammation, and the presence of contralateral aspiration pneumonia were strongly associated with survival after open-window thoracostomy. items.title: MRI acute/sub-acute ischemic stroke segmentation with deep learning: A comprehensive review
items.description: The segmentation of ischemic stroke lesions from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) images using deep learning (DL) techniques has emerged as a critical area of research in medical imaging. items.title: Thoracic Endovascular Repair for Retrograde Type A Aortic Dissection: True Lumen Device Sizing
items.description: CONCLUSIONS: TEVAR for retrograde type A aortic dissection is a feasible strategy with acceptable mid- to long-term outcomes in selected patients.
